---
title: "Refactor Your App Into a Module"
---

## Where we're starting

The tips dashboard's sidebar today: one slider (`total_bill`), one checkbox
group (`time`), one `@reactive.calc filtered_data()`. All hand-written,
all tied to the `tips` dataframe specifically.

## Step 1 — identify the reusable part

- The sidebar UI (slider + checkbox group)
- The filtering logic (`@reactive.calc`)
- Everything *else* (value boxes, plots, tables) stays in the app — it's
  specific to how *this* app wants to display the filtered data

## Step 2 — write the module

We already built this in the last section:
`filter_panel(input, output, session, data, columns)` — pass in any
dataframe and any list of columns, get back `{"mask": <reactive.calc>}`.

## Step 3 — swap the sidebar for the module

Before:

```{.python}
with ui.sidebar(open="desktop"):
    ui.input_slider(id="slider", label="Bill amount", ...)
    ui.input_checkbox_group(id="checkbox_group", label="Food service", ...)

@reactive.calc
def filtered_data():
    idx1 = tips.total_bill.between(...)
    idx2 = tips.time.isin(...)
    return tips[idx1 & idx2]
```

After:

```{.python}
with ui.sidebar():
    tips_filter = filter_panel(
        "tips_filter", data=tips, columns=["total_bill", "time"]
    )
```

Everywhere the app used `filtered_data()`, it now uses
`tips.loc[tips_filter["mask"]()]`.

## The refactored app

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/demos/app-06-modules_refactor-express.py >}}

## file: filter_module_express.py
{{< include /../code/demos/filter_module_express.py >}}
```

## What we dropped

The original app had a "Reset filter" button. We left it out of the module
to keep this refactor focused — adding it back is your exercise.

## Exercise

See exercise: [Refactor into a Module](../exercises.qmd#ex-modules-refactor).
